articles.title: "We're Negotiating Now": Trump Jokingly Calls Hormuz "Strait Of Trump"
articles.description: US President Donald Trump on Friday insisted Iran must open up the Strait of Hormuz to oil traffic to make a peace deal -- and dubbed the crucial waterway the "Strait of Trump."

articles.title: Amit Shah's Kolkata flight delayed by two hours due to thunderstorm
articles.description: According to airport sources, the special aircraft carrying the home minister from Delhi was scheduled to land at 11:46 pm.

topStories.title: Tiger Woods Arrested for DUI in Florida Rollover Car Crash
topStories.description: Tiger Woods was arrested Friday afternoon for driving under the influence after crashing his vehicle into another car in Jupiter Island, Florida, the Martin County Sheriff’s Office said.

topStories.title: Houthis warn ‘fingers on the trigger’ as US-Israel war on Iran continues
topStories.description: The Iran-aligned Yemeni group have the ability to target key shipping lanes around the Arabian Peninsula.

topStories.title: "We're Negotiating Now": Trump Jokingly Calls Hormuz "Strait Of Trump"
topStories.description: US President Donald Trump on Friday insisted Iran must open up the Strait of Hormuz to oil traffic to make a peace deal -- and dubbed the crucial waterway the "Strait of Trump."

topStories.title: ‘Fingers on the trigger’ for military intervention, Houthis warn
topStories.description: The Houthis have warned that they are ready for direct military intervention in the US-Israeli war on Iran.

topStories.title: Tiger Woods charged with DUI after rollover crash in Florida
topStories.description: Tiger Woods showed signs of impairment and was arrested at the scene of car crash in which he struck another vehicle.

topStories.title: 'My daughter is under the rubble': Inside Tehran as civilian toll of strikes rises
topStories.description: One month since the US and Israel launched strikes on Iran, Tehran residents tell the BBC their lives have been devastated.

topStories.title: Tiger Woods charged with driving under influence after crash
topStories.description: Golfer Tiger Woods is charged with driving under the influence after a car crash in Florida, police say.

topStories.title: Guns in the streets as US, Israel intensify month-long attacks across Iran
topStories.description: A senior IRGC official in Tehran told state television that children over 12 can join armed patrols and checkpoints.

topStories.title: Rubio says US expects to finish Iran war 'in next couple of weeks'
topStories.description: Marco Rubio said US allies were open to helping escort ships through the Strait of Hormuz, which Iran has closed and threatened to charge fees for.

topStories.title: US Hopes For Iran Meetings "This Week" To End Month-Long War: Trump Envoy
topStories.description: US President Trump's special envoy Steve Witkoff said Friday he believes Iran will hold talks with Washington "this week" to end the month-long war in the Middle East.

topStories.title: Mexico’s navy searches for two missing boats involved in Cuba aid convoy
topStories.description: The Nuestra America Convoy said that the sailboats were led by 'experienced sailors' with access to safety equipment.

dailyNews.title: Supreme Court flags repeated breaches of law on disclosure of sexual assault survivors’ identity
dailyNews.description: Supreme Court warns of “general indifference” as case records reveal disclosure of sexual assault survivors’ identity, violating IPC Section 228A

dailyNews.title: BJD demands VAT reduction on petrol, diesel in Odisha
dailyNews.description: BJD spokesperson Lenin Mohanty said the ongoing conflict involving Israel, the United States and Iran has triggered concerns of a global fuel crisis

dailyNews.title: Karnataka bypolls: Congress rebel candidate retires from contest in Davangere South
dailyNews.description: Rebel candidate Sadiq Pahilwan announced that he would extend full support to Congress candidate Samarth Mallikarjun, son of Minister S.S. Mallikarjun

dailyNews.title: US Palestinian activist says FBI foiled assassination plot against her
dailyNews.description: US law enforcement say man was arrested for plotting Molotov cocktail attack against activist Nerdeen Kiswani.

dailyNews.title: Memory chip giant SK hynix could help end ‘RAMmageddon’ with blockbuster US IPO
dailyNews.description: SK hynix’s potential U.S. listing could raise $10-$14 billion to help it build more capacity, encourage others to follow, and end the 'RAMmageddon' memory shortage.

topStories.title: Trump reacts to Tiger Woods' rollover crash as golfer faces DUI charge: ‘Very close friend of mine’
topStories.description: Tiger Woods was arrested on a DUI charge weeks before the Masters, with President Donald Trump calling the 15-time major winner a close friend in response to the car crash.

topStories.description: Politico reported that ICE acting Director Todd Lyons was hospitalized for stress linked to Trump's deportation push, but the White House calls it trash reporting.

topStories.description: Joe Rogan and Dave Smith argue Trump's Iran war has shattered his historic 2024 coalition, and Smith warned it could hand the country back to Democrats.

dailyNews.description: Joe Rogan and Dave Smith argue Trump's Iran war has shattered his historic 2024 coalition, and Smith warned it could hand the country back to Democrats.

dailyNews.title: WATCH: Trump goes viral for illustrating how to cut government waste with his favorite White House pen
dailyNews.description: President Donald Trump went on a viral Cabinet meeting tangent about a favorite White House custom Sharpie that he said shows how he can get “better" results for less cost.

dailyNews.title: White House blasts POLITICO report on acting ICE chief hospitalizations as ‘inaccurate trash’
dailyNews.description: A report claiming acting ICE Director Todd Lyons was hospitalized multiple times for stress draws a sharp response from the White House, which denied the allegations and emphasized his role in advancing immigration policy.

dailyNews.title: 'Dukes of Hazzard' star John Schneider says Democrats secretly rely on Trump policies they publicly attack
dailyNews.description: "Dukes of Hazzard" star John Schneider accused Democrats of benefiting from Trump's policies while opposing them, calling the party an "embarrassment."

dailyNews.title: When Will TSA Workers Be Paid? Here’s What We Know
dailyNews.description: President Trump on Friday directed the Homeland Security Department to pay thousands of airport security officers.

dailyNews.title: At CPAC, the ‘America First’ Crowd Ponders Giving War a Chance
dailyNews.description: The Conservative Political Action Conference has been an America First gathering for years, but with President Trump pursuing war in Iran, organizers have tried to make a shift, with mixed results.

dailyNews.title: Outside Kennedy Center, Jane Fonda and Joan Baez Raise Voices in Protest
dailyNews.description: The pair joined in a gathering of artists and others who denounced censorship and faulted President Trump’s growing influence over the nation’s cultural life.

dailyNews.title: Kennedy Center Performer Asks Judge to Toss Case He Calls ‘Retaliatory’
dailyNews.description: A jazz drummer who called off a performance in protest of the center’s being renamed for President Trump said the president’s allies had sued him as punishment.

dailyNews.title: One Republican Farmer in Wisconsin Is Bitter Over Trump’s Deportations
dailyNews.description: For 20 years, Tim O’Harrow has asked lawmakers to acknowledge that America’s food supply depends on immigrants. Politics went in the other direction.

dailyNews.title: Iran war rages, Strait of Hormuz still blocked as Trump pushes for a deal
dailyNews.description: The Trump administration expects Iran's formal response to its 15-point peace proposal today, as Tehran continues blocking the Strait of Hormuz.

dailyNews.title: Iran claims Strait of Hormuz is closed as Trump says talks are underway
dailyNews.description: According to a statement from Iran's Revolutionary Guards, the Strait of Hormuz is closed, and they vowed a severe response to any ship trying to get through. That seemed to contradict President Trump's claim that Iran had allowed 10 ships through the Strait as a gift. Holly Williams has details.

dailyNews.title: House GOP attempts to forge own path toward ending shutdown, disregarding Senate-backed bill
dailyNews.description: House lawmakers are at odds over a Senate bill that includes TSA pay, but excludes ICE funding. Eleanor Mueller, White House economic policy reporter for Semafor, and Riley Rogerson, House leadership reporter for Politico, join "The Takeout" to discuss.

dailyNews.title: What methods is Trump using to try to negotiate with Iran?
dailyNews.description: The U.S. is waiting for Iran's response to a 15-point outline of a peace deal that was presented on Thursday, with Pakistan acting as an intermediary. CBS News national security contributor Sam Vinograd has more.
